title: Add S3 lifecycle policy for console-contexts prefix

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
## Scope

Configure S3 bucket lifecycle to auto-expire console context audit artifacts after 14 days.

## Implementation

Add lifecycle rule to reportblockdetails bucket (or env-specific equivalent):
- Rule name: `console-contexts-expiration`
- Prefix filter: `console-contexts/`
- Expiration: 14 days
- Apply to current versions

This balances observability needs (recent debugging) with storage costs.

## Configuration

Update Terraform/CloudFormation/AWS CLI configuration for S3 bucket.

## Testing

Manual verification:
- Upload test artifact to `console-contexts/test/artifact.json`
- Verify lifecycle policy shows 14-day expiration
- Confirm no impact on other prefixes (reportblocks/, procedures/)

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
## IMPORTANT: Infrastructure as Code

All AWS resource changes must be done through IaC (Terraform/CDK/CloudFormation), not manual Console changes.

## Scope

Configure S3 bucket lifecycle to auto-expire console context audit artifacts after 14 days.

## Implementation

Update IaC configuration for reportblockdetails bucket (or env-specific equivalent):
- Add lifecycle rule via Terraform/CDK resource definition
- Rule name: `console-contexts-expiration`
- Prefix filter: `console-contexts/`
- Expiration: 14 days
- Apply to current versions

## Files to Modify

Find and update the IaC definition for the S3 bucket. Likely locations:
- `infrastructure/terraform/` or similar
- CDK stack definitions
- CloudFormation templates

## Testing

After IaC deployment:
- Verify lifecycle policy applied via AWS CLI: `aws s3api get-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket <bucket-name>`
- Upload test artifact to `console-contexts/test/artifact.json`
- Verify expiration date set correctly
- Confirm no impact on other prefixes (reportblocks/, procedures/)

## Deployment

Changes must go through normal IaC deployment pipeline (terraform apply, cdk deploy, etc).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
## Files to Modify

The S3 bucket for report blocks is likely managed via AWS Amplify. Check:
- `dashboard/amplify/storage/resource.ts` - Amplify storage resource definition
- `infrastructure/stacks/*_stack.py` - CDK stacks that might define the bucket

Add lifecycle configuration to the storage bucket resource definition.

For Amplify Gen 2, lifecycle rules are added via the `defineStorage()` configuration.
